prompt: Photorealistic construction site photography. Adelaide CBD construction hoarding panels in dark charcoal (#2C2C2C) colour, stretching along a city street. The large logo dominates the hoarding — a pentagon shape formed by taking a rectangle and slicing off only the top-right corner at a 45-degree diagonal, creating a five-sided shape (flat bottom, flat left side, flat top until three-quarters across, then a diagonal cut down to the right side, flat right side). This pentagon shape is rendered in a slightly lighter charcoal tone against the dark panels. The letters LK are displayed in gold (#C9A96E) within the pentagon shape, appearing carved/inset in an elegant serif font. A thin gold line along the bottom inside the shape. Below reads DESIGN AND BUILD in refined small capitals with generous letter-spacing. The solid geometric pentagon shape commands presence at large scale. Adelaide streetscape visible, pedestrians walking past for scale. Shot on 24mm wide angle lens, overcast light, cinematic, premium construction company, urban environment.
